title: Was Keels body regrown like an Evas
display_title: Was Keels body regrown like an Evas
wikitext:
[]: Was Keel's body regrown like an Eva's?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 08, 2010 1:38 am There was previously a thread about why Keel is a cyborg in the Chit-chat section, but I think what I have to say is Discussion-worthy. Merge it if it suits your fancy. In Neon Genesis Evangelion , Keel Lorenz is always seen "seated", and we never see any part of him below the torso. http://projectenthalpy.com/lorenz.jpg Looking more closely at Keel's remains after he liquifies, it's clear that, by 2015, he did not look human below the waist. Whatever the reason for his cyborgization, it was so extensive that it appears he was immobile. No legs are visible, and his spinal cord keeps going long past the point at which a human spine should end. For all we know, his spine and the other tubes keep going for yards past the edge of the screen. All of those countless gray and blue tubes must be plugged into something, meaning he is connected to another machine of some sort. A machine which may or may not have looked something like this. This sort of surgery seems insanely complex even for the Eva universe. It would involve removing all of his body below the torso, all of his internal organs below the head, including his lungs and his heart, and all of his bones except those in his head and arms. Keel's skin was still organic, but absolutely everything in his torso below the skin and maybe some muscle was scooped out . What an insane procedure that would be, scooping out all the bones and organs from inside the torso. So insane, in fact, that I don't believe it ever happened. Removing the heart and lungs would result in a quick death. Keel would be dead by the time they got all his insides out of his torso just to make room for the mechanical parts. Remember, this wouldn't be a simple orthotopic procedure: everything from his ribcage on in would be removed. Surviving that would be impossible. I don't think an operation of that sort ever happened. An entirely different procedure occurred. Not quite like this one, but closer to this than to traditional surgery. Here's what probably happened: Sometime after Second Impact, Keel Lorenz was either dying or he actually died. Whatever caused this, most of his body was destroyed. He was too important to Seele to lose, however, so his body was "regrown". His head may have been his original head, and his arms may be his original arms as well, but the rest of him was regenerated around his mechanical parts. Or maybe what we see of him was entirely regenerated and his soul was harvested from his old body, which is now rotting somewhere. Regardless of which way it happened, Keel spent some time dead. Even if the preposterous scooping-out-the-insides-and-hooking-the-mechanical-parts-up-piece-by-piece process did occur, Keel must have been dead at some point during the operation as he would have lacked a heart and lungs for a good length of time before his new mechanical insides were attached in a manner that could sustain him. In case anyone cares or it becomes relevant, Grigorievich Rodonaia apparently holds the record for being dead the longest time before returning to life. He was dead for three days. I can't find any source on this information or proof that whoever declared him dead was just incompetent, however, so I'm going to assume he was never quite dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jul 08, 2010 1:49 am ...

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 08, 2010 1:53 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: reasoning that he himself died because of things such as hearts and lungs being missing for even a few moments hardly does anime cybernetics justice

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 08, 2010 2:02 am NAveryW wrote: YEAH WELL JUST FOR THAT YOU DON'T GET TO READ THE REST OF WHAT I WAS GOING TO SAY.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Thu Jul 08, 2010 2:03 am NAveryW wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 08, 2010 2:32 am (Edit: Apparently jesting a little--espeically past midnight--is to be avoided. Mmkay.) Similiar to the issuse with Kaworu's donor, Kiel's problems just aren't considered "important" enough to address somewhere in-series, it seems. Just a hunch. BUT, whatever the hell happened to Kiel could have been Second Impact related. He looks healthy as well as actually standing across from Gendo in the flashback photo(s). It was part of some quick flash as Fuyutsuki narrated in episode 21', I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 08, 2010 8:43 am I don't know who locked this thread with no headsup, but it IS supposed to be serious despite some humor at first (note the fact the second half of my post gave actual input on what happened with Kiel). Naw, please remove that first image since it came across as too jokey. And let's all try this again and keep things on-topic . Edit : Fixed the topic title.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jul 08, 2010 10:10 am Whoever changed the topic did it wrong. There's no way to tell why Keel became a cyborg and that's not what I intended the thread to be about. The thread's about the idea that Keel's body was "regrown" around his mechanical insides in a manner similar to that in which Evas are grown, as the alternative is having all his insides scooped out.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Thu Jul 08, 2010 10:39 am So change it to something else.

[]: Fullmetal Alkeelmist.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Jul 08, 2010 11:43 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: BUT, whatever the hell happened to Kiel could have been Second Impact related. He looks healthy as well as actually standing across from Gendo in the flashback photo(s). It was part of some quick flash as Fuyutsuki narrated in episode 21', I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 08, 2010 11:52 am Y'know, Keel's lower body reminds me of Tunneliel more than anything else. Maybe he was trying to get his soul transferred to an Evangelion, but failed? (Also, what SSD and Zap said about that dinosaur and the thread title)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 08, 2010 12:25 pm The image of Kiel's remains is a big mess... but maybe these will help. BTW: http://www.evacommentary.org/episode-m26/m26_C342_comp.jpg http://www.evacommentary.org/episode-m26/m26_C342_col-disc.jpg http://www.evacommentary.org/images_production/groundwork_m26_C342.gif (I wonder how his remains are laying flat on the floor when he was seated and appear to have dropped straight down from there...) Here is Kiel -- he was at least designed to superficially look like a normal human being (visor aside). The spine does look incredibly long in the "cyborg leftovers" image, but FWIW the part that's poking up at the bottom looks like the lumbar section. Perhaps the spinal column snapped in half on the tumble down. As for how Kiel got the way he did: Some kind of degenerative condition has been forwarded. This would result in his parts gradually being replaced, until finally little more than his head, arms, and skin are left. The cybernetization itself would be inelegant and improvised, since they're basically winging it as more and more of his body fails. Most of his body being destroyed as a result of some unknown incident is the one I tend to go with, though. Having soft tissues generated over his new cyborg parts seems plausible enough.

[]: Nothing like fanwanking over some old man's spine.             Quote Post by Killer Bee » Thu Jul 08, 2010 12:38 pm Reichu wrote: The image of Kiel's remains is a big mess...

An entirely different procedure occurred. Here's what probably happened: Sometime after Second Impact, Keel Lorenz was either dying or he actually died. Whatever caused this, most of his body was destroyed. He was too important to Seele to lose, however, so his body was "regrown". His head may have been his original head, and his arms may be his original arms as well, but the rest of him was regenerated around his mechanical parts. Or maybe what we see of him was entirely regenerated and his soul was harvested from his old body, which is now rotting somewhere.

[]: Re: Nothing like fanwanking over some old man's spine.             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 08, 2010 12:52 pm Killer Bee wrote: Literally. But, wait... if Keel tanged, shouldn't his spine have tanged, too? Why would it be the only natural part of his body left behind? Unless it's actually just another mechanical piece designed to resemble a normal spine(which would also explain why it's so damn long).

[]: Re: Nothing like fanwanking over some old man's spine.             Quote Post by Dark doom » Thu Jul 08, 2010 12:59 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Maybe his spine is PWM. It's simply way too damn long.
